content.summary: Ibex Medical Analytics (ibex-ai.com) is an Israel-founded, Boston-headquartered AI healthtech company specializing in clinical-grade AI for cancer diagnostics in pathology. Founded in 2016 (incubated at Kamet Ventures), the company is the most widely deployed AI platform in pathology globally, processing whole-slide images to detect and grade breast, prostate, and gastric cancers. As of July 2026, Ibex has raised over $100M across 7 rounds, with the most recent being a $55M Series C (September 2023) led by 83North, joined by Sienna Venture Capital, Octopus Ventures, aMoon, Planven Entrepreneur Ventures, and Dell Technologies Capital. The company has ~97 employees (51–200 range). In February 2026, Ibex completed a leadership transition appointing Yair Heller (formerly COO) as CEO, while co-founder Joseph Mossel assumed the President & GM U.S. role focusing on biopharma growth. Key technical leadership includes CTO and co-founder Chaim Linhart, PhD. The platform (now branded Ibex 4) runs on AWS cloud infrastructure with deep learning algorithms trained on 10M+ slides, integrating with partners including Philips, Roche navify, Proscia, and FUJIFILM via open API into LIS and IMS systems. Security certifications include HITRUST, ISO 27001, ISO 13485, and Cyber Essentials Plus. In February 2025, Ibex received its first FDA 510(k) clearance (for Ibex Prostate Detect). Strong buying signals include the new CEO driving global scale execution, the Ibex 4 platform launch (March 2026), 70%+ customer base growth, active biopharma division expansion, new U.S. lab partnerships, and ongoing hiring across Israel and the U.S.
content.industry: Hospitals and Health Care — AI-powered cancer diagnostics / computational pathology
tech_stack: Proprietary deep learning / computer vision AI platform (Ibex 4 / Galen platform) trained on 10M+ pathology slides
Amazon Web Services (AWS) — cloud infrastructure for scalable slide image processing
Open API for LIS (Laboratory Information System) and IMS (Image Management System) integrations
Philips IntelliSite Pathology Solution — clinical integration partner
Roche navify Digital Pathology — integration partner
ISO 13485-compliant QMS; HITRUST, ISO 27001, Cyber Essentials Plus certifications
content.headquarters: 17 Station Street, Suite 203, Brookline, Massachusetts 02445, USA (also Tel Aviv, Israel and London, UK)

key_contacts.name: Richard Nicholson
key_contacts.title: Head of Business Development & Partnerships, Europe

content.total_funding: >$100 million (7 rounds since 2016; most recent: $55M Series C, September 2023)
buying_signals: Leadership change (February 2026): New CEO Yair Heller installed; co-founder Joseph Mossel pivoting to biopharma and U.S. clinical growth — new strategic priorities signal fresh budget cycles and vendor re-evaluation.

Major platform launch (March 2026): Ibex 4 next-generation AI platform released with expanded breast, prostate, and gastric workflows — active platform roll-out creates LIS/IMS integration, implementation, and services needs.
Rapid commercial expansion: Doubled case volume for the second consecutive year in 2025; customer base grew 70%+; 3x new applications deployed vs. 2024.
New U.S. clinical partnerships (2026): HNL Lab Medicine (March 2026) and PathGroup — active U.S. market expansion signals sales infrastructure and vendor partner investment.

Biopharma division launch: New biopharma advisory board (May 2026) and early biomarker development service expansion (January 2026) — new business line requiring distinct clinical, regulatory, and data infrastructure.
Active hiring: Open roles across Israel and U.S. (Senior Product Manager, Application Engineers, BD roles) — headcount growth signals budget availability.
FDA 510(k) clearance (February 2025) for Ibex Prostate Detect — first FDA clearance unlocks U.S. clinical sales; active U.S. commercialization underway.
Technology migration: Ibex 4 platform supersedes prior 'Galen' platform — existing lab customers migrating to new platform, creating workflow integration and onboarding opportunities.
Strategic tech partnership with AWS for cloud-native digital pathology scaling — signals cloud infrastructure investment and partner ecosystem expansion.
Active conference presence and European expansion roadshow (ECDP 2026, USCAP 2026, France roadshow 2026) — strong go-to-market activity indicates sales cycle acceleration.
content.headcount_estimate: 51–200 employees (LinkedIn: ~97 employees listed, as of July 2026)
